semanticCore: Retribution, vengeance, dislike, punishment, or anger
quranicRelevance: Retribution/punishment from God (Intiqam - 'fantaqamna minhum'), avenging wrongdoers, and disliking/resenting ('wa ma naqamoo').

keyDerivations.meaning: To resent, dislike, disapprove, or avenge
To take vengeance, avenge, or punish

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَمَا تَنْقِمُ مِنَّا إِلَّا أَنْ آمَنَّا (7:126): And you do not resent/dislike from us except that we believed (prejudice/hostility).
discardedNoise: Obscure idioms on eating meat greedily.
summaryEn: The root represents retribution and resentment, detailing the moral hostility of the wicked (Naqam) and the divine justice of retribution (Intiqam).
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الانتقام والإنكار، واصفاً المخاصمة والعداء المعنوي للحق (نقم) والعقاب الإلهي العادل للظالمين (انتقام).
rawText: اِنْتَقَمْتُ مِنْهُ 8 I took, or executed, vengeance on him, or inflicted penal retribution on him, for that which he had done: (JK:) or I punished him; (S, Msb, K;) as also 🡻نَقَمْتُمِنْهُ, (Msb, K,) and عَلَيهِ, (TA,) aor. ﹻ; (Msb, K;) and نَقِمْتُ. (K.) === See نِقْمَةٌ. نَقَمَ عَلَيْهِ 1 He exacted vengeance upon him, punished him: see an ex. voce ابدى in art. بدو. See 8. نِقْمَةٌ [and 🡻اِنْتِقَامٌ] Vengeance; or penal retribution. (JK.)
